title: nymphaea odorata subsp. gigantea
intro: Nymphaea odorata subsp. gigantea, also known as the giant fragrant water lily or giant water lily, is a species of aquatic plant that belongs to the Nymphaea genus. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its roots and rhizomes. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. The plant's roots and rhizomes are often used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
